content.text_content:
MONTZKA EYE CLINIC - AMBULATORY SURGERY CENTER
OPERATIVE NOTE

Patient: James E. Whitfield
DOB: 06/30/1953
MRN: MEC-2022-0388
Date of Surgery: December 5, 2024
Surgeon: Elena Montzka, MD
Anesthesia: Topical with intravenous sedation

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Visually significant nuclear sclerotic cataract, right eye (H25.11)
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same
PROCEDURE: Phacoemulsification with posterior chamber intraocular lens implantation, right eye (CPT 66984)
IMPLANT: Alcon AcrySof SN60WF, 21.0 diopters, placed in the capsular bag

DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE:
After informed consent, the patient was brought to the operating suite and the right eye was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion. A temporal clear corneal incision was created, the anterior chamber was filled with viscoelastic, and a continuous curvilinear capsulorhexis was performed. The nucleus was disassembled and removed by phacoemulsification, and cortical material was aspirated. The intraocular lens was injected into the capsular bag and centered. Viscoelastic was removed, the incision was hydrated and confirmed watertight, and intracameral antibiotic was given. The procedure was uncomplicated and the patient tolerated it well.

POSTOPERATIVE INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Moxifloxacin 0.5% one drop right eye three times daily for one week.
2. Prednisolone acetate 1% one drop right eye four times daily, taper per follow-up schedule.
3. Eye shield at night for one week. No heavy lifting or eye rubbing.
4. Follow-up in clinic tomorrow, at one week, and at three weeks.

[ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE APPLIED]
Elena Montzka, MD
Dictated: 12/05/2024 12:10 | Signed: 12/05/2024 12:25

procedure.name: Phacoemulsification with posterior chamber IOL implantation

content.text_content:
MONTZKA EYE CLINIC - OFFICE VISIT NOTE

Patient: James E. Whitfield
DOB: 06/30/1953
MRN: MEC-2022-0388
Date of Service: December 12, 2024
Visit Type: Established Patient - Post-operative Week 1
Provider: Elena Montzka, MD

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
One-week post-operative check, right eye cataract surgery.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr. Whitfield is seven days out from uncomplicated phacoemulsification with intraocular lens implantation of the right eye. He is very pleased with his vision and reports no pain, redness, or new floaters. He is using his drops as directed.

EXAMINATION:
Visual acuity (uncorrected): OD 20/25, OS 20/40
Intraocular pressure (applanation): OD 14 mmHg, OS 15 mmHg
Right eye: cornea clear, anterior chamber deep and quiet with rare cell, incisions sealed, intraocular lens well centered in the capsular bag
Dilated fundus examination OD: macula flat, no cystoid edema, periphery attached

ASSESSMENT:
1. Status post uncomplicated phacoemulsification with intraocular lens, right eye (12/05/2024) - excellent early result.

PLAN:
1. Discontinue moxifloxacin at the end of this week.
2. Continue prednisolone acetate 1% right eye: three times daily this week, then twice daily for one week, then once daily for one week, then stop.
3. Return in two weeks for the three-week post-operative check with macular OCT; refraction for updated glasses at that visit.

[ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE APPLIED]
Elena Montzka, MD
Dictated: 12/12/2024 11:40 | Signed: 12/12/2024 11:55
